title: Prompting, Structured Outputs, and Tool Calling
shortSummary: Design prompts that are testable, typed, and operationally useful instead of vague chat instructions.
whyItMatters: Frontier teams care far less about clever prompt hacks than about reproducible prompting patterns, typed outputs, and safe tool invocation. This is the bridge from toy prompt work to product-grade behavior.
coreConcepts: Prompt scaffolds for classification, extraction, and planning
JSON schemas and contract-first model outputs
Tool selection, arguments, retries, and approval boundaries
Failure handling for malformed or partial responses
Prompt iteration with evals instead of vibes
